pattern: Almost every 2x2 block is entirely white, so instead of scanning the whole grid it is enough to visit the handful of blocks that actually touch a colored cell and tally their counts in a map.
hints: A colored cell at (x, y) can only ever be a corner of up to four 2x2 blocks -- the ones whose top-left corner is (x-1,y-1), (x-1,y), (x,y-1) or (x,y), whichever of those fall inside the grid.
Keep a running tally, keyed by a block's top-left corner, of how many colored cells have landed in it so far; only blocks that appear in this tally can have a non-zero count.
Once every colored cell has been distributed, the number of blocks with zero colored cells is the total block count minus however many distinct blocks ended up in the tally.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible grid, no colored cells
smallest possible grid, every cell colored
larger grid with no colored cells at all
an entire top row colored, splitting neighboring blocks evenly
a full narrow grid colored so every block has all four corners black
scattered cells that overlap the same block from different corners

stress.description: random sparse coloring on a moderately sized grid
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(2, min(size, 1000))
    n = max(2, min(size, 1000))
    k = rng.randint(0, min(10000, m * n))
    seen = set()
    coords = []
    attempts = 0
    while len(coords) < k and attempts < k * 20 + 100:
        attempts += 1
        x = rng.randint(0, m - 1)
        y = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if (x, y) not in seen:
            seen.add((x, y))
            coords.append([x, y])
    return [m, n, coords]
